stories.headline: Oil Surges as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Threaten Fragile Truce
stories.summary: Oil jumped after the US started new strikes on Iran, with the Islamic Republic responding by announcing a halt to all vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, putting further strain on a fragile ceasefire.

stories.headline: ‘Negotiate with bombs’: Hegseth defends second night of US strikes on Iran
stories.summary: US Central Command has described the attacks as a result of 'Iran’s unwarranted and continued aggression'.

stories.headline: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons in visit to Guantánamo Bay
stories.summary: US defense secretary continues ramp-up of pressure against country including sanctions and devastating oil blockadePete Hegseth has warned Cuba against acquiring weapons that could threaten the United States, during a visit to the US military base at Guantánamo Bay.Washington has ramped up pressure against Cuba with sanctions and a devastating oil blockade, and Donald Trump has repeatedly signaled that the Cuban government could be the next after

stories.headline: Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
stories.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
stories.summary: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

stories.headline: US launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Tehran announces Strait of Hormuz will be closed ‘until further notice’ in response to American operation

stories.headline: US Launches Second Round of Strikes on Iran
stories.summary: The US military launched strikes against "multiple" targets in Iran for the second day in a row after President Donald Trump accused the country of dragging out talks on an interim peace deal. Bloomberg's Laura Davison explains the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Kyiv hit Russian military plant using Ukrainian-made Flamingo missile, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy vowed to continue Kyiv’s strikes deep inside Russia in response to attacks on Ukraine, but also a way of forcing the Kremlin into direct talks.

stories.summary: Trump warns of more attacks on Iran after US Apache helicopter downing

stories.headline: US military chief Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring military arms
stories.summary: Hegseth's visit to Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, comes as the Trump administration increases pressure against Cuba's government.

stories.headline: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
stories.summary: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North SumatraExtreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival.The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans (Pongo tapanuliensis) were kil

stories.headline: French star Patrick Bruel charged with rape and sexual assault
stories.summary: Singer and actor has denied all charges after more than 20 women came forward with allegations going back to the 1990sThe French singer Patrick Bruel has been charged with rape and sexual assault in one of the biggest #MeToo cases in the French music industry.The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture, was placed under formal investigation over several cases that included alleged rape, attempted rape, sexual assault and sexual harassme

stories.headline: Deadly clashes in Kenya as protesters fight US Ebola quarantine facility
stories.summary: Kenya saw fresh unrest on 9 June as protesters defied a court order and clashed with police near Laikipia Air Base over plans for a US funded Ebola quarantine facility, fuelling fears about the virus and prompting arrests.

stories.headline: Bill Gates says Epstein wanted personal relationship, but he 'never reciprocated'
stories.summary: The Microsoft co-founder spent hours talking to lawmakers behind closed doors, where he also said Epstein used his marital infidelities to pressure him.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

stories.headline: French singer Patrick Bruel faces rape charges
stories.summary: Bruel has been placed under judicial investigation, which means an examining magistrate will look more deeply into the case.

stories.headline: Ukraine says missiles hit military plant deep inside Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine's military also says it has struck a Russian oil refinery and a "shadow fleet" oil tanker in the Black Sea.

stories.headline: US tech stocks sink as volatility flares up on Wall Street
stories.summary: Latest bout of selling comes ahead of SpaceX’s historic market debut

stories.headline: Man Accused of $37 Billion LA Fire Is Innocent, His Lawyer Says
stories.summary: Jonathan Rinderknecht has been wrongly accused of starting the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history, his lawyer told a jury.

stories.headline: Trump Officials Say ICE Won’t Raid World Cup Games, but Fans Are Worried
stories.summary: Federal agents are there for security, not immigration enforcement, officials say. But trust is fragile after the administration’s recent crackdowns.

stories.headline: FISA Surveillance Law May Expire After Trump Picks Bill Pulte for Intel Post
stories.summary: Republicans are struggling to extend a powerful surveillance authority set to lapse this weekend after President Trump alienated lawmakers with his choice of acting spy chief.

stories.headline: Australia news live: minister warns about impact of datacentre boom; Seven farewells ‘smart, talented’ people in major staff cuts
stories.summary: Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWong not ‘interested’ in One Nation’s fundraisingWong says she isn’t concerned about One Nation’s fundraising efforts, but more about their policies. Pauline Hanson’s party says it has raised more than $1.5m in the last day, although those claims are unverified.I’m less interested in what Pauline Hanson fundraises and am more concerned about One Nation’s policies.

stories.headline: Trial begins for man accused of sparking Palisades Fire in California
stories.summary: Prosecutors say man deliberately started blaze that became one of the most destructive wildfires in Los Angeles history.

stories.headline: Israeli strike on a car kills two in Sidon, Lebanon

stories.headline: Bill Gates testifies in closed-door US House meeting over Epstein ties

stories.headline: Hong Kong charges seven people and two firms over massive fire that killed 168
stories.summary: The massive blaze, which engulfed seven of the eight high-rise apartment blocks at the Wang Fuk Court estate in November, prompted a months-long investigation into the cause.

stories.headline: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman
stories.summary: India's government said three Indian sailors were missing and 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

stories.headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
stories.summary: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

stories.headline: Growing backlash in Japan over Trump's use of anime characters

stories.headline: Alleged Bondi Beach gunman charged with another 19 offences
stories.summary: Naveed Akram, 24, is already facing 59 charges over an attack on a Jewish festival in December.

stories.headline: What happened to the great rebirth of American manufacturing?
stories.summary: Skills shortages, shifting tariffs and complex permitting processes are major barriers

stories.headline: Oracle to spend $70bn on data centre build-out in coming year

stories.headline: Westpac Mortgage Applications Fall as Tax Changes Sap Demand
stories.summary: Westpac Banking Corp. mortgage applications are heading for the worst quarter in a year as tax changes for property investors weaken demand for real estate.

stories.headline: Highlights from Bloomberg Invest Hong Kong
stories.summary: Top voices in finance joined Bloomberg to discuss topics ranging from the impact of China's regulatory crackdown to the risk of a global tech selloff. Bloomberg's David Ingles and Yvonne Man break down the highlights. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Global Citizen CEO on First-Ever FIFA Halftime Show
stories.summary: Hugh Evans, CEO of Global Citizen, joined the program to discuss the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlighting the first-ever halftime show for the tournament. Curated by Chris Martin of Coldplay, the halftime event will feature performances by Madonna, BTS, and Shakira, and is expected to be the most viewed halftime show in history. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Treasury Eases Legal Restrictions Across Venezuela Licenses
stories.summary: The Trump administration amended its Venezuela licenses to loosen US-related legal provisions as it seeks to facilitate investment in the country’s oil and other natural resources.

stories.headline: Chevron Among Drillers to Feed Argentina Shale NGL Venture
stories.summary: Chevron Corp. and two other major Argentine shale producers will sign contracts this week to supply a natural gas liquids project, a move that all but assures the $3 billion plan goes ahead, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Stock Investors Eye Deep Run for Japan at Football World Cup
stories.summary: Japan enters the 2026 football World Cup as Asia’s top-ranked team, fueling hopes that the Samurai Blue can finally reach the quarterfinals and lift shares of streaming, food and beverage companies along the way.

stories.headline: Oracle Earnings Report | Closing Bell
stories.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Cancer Drugmaker Parabilis Surges 58% in US Trading Debut
stories.summary: Parabilis Medicines Inc. shares jumped 58% in its trading debut, after the company raised nearly $745 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced above its marketed range and a private placement.

stories.headline: Graham Platner’s Victory in Maine Senate Primary Gives Democrats Hope and Fears
stories.summary: Graham Platner’s primary victory sets up a high-stakes contest between an insurgent progressive with political baggage and a battle-tested but vulnerable Republican senator.

stories.headline: Nancy Mace’s thrashing in South Carolina governor’s race caps a rough downfall
stories.summary: The congresswoman finished fifth in the state’s GOP gubernatorial primary, according to unofficial returns, solidly losing even her own home county and district.

stories.headline: The vanishing of Nicolás Maduro: how the former dictator is being erased from Venezuela

stories.headline: Man reportedly shot at Kenya protest against US Ebola quarantine centre

stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.summary: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plansKenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens.Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.

stories.headline: Israeli air strikes hit Lebanese city of Tyre despite Iranian warning to stop attacks
stories.summary: Iran warned Israel on Monday that it could resume hostilities if attacks on its Lebanese ally Hezbollah do not stop.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Monsoon season brings vital rainfall to parts of Asia
stories.summary: India declares onset as up to 280mm of rain falls in 72 hours in Kerala, while downpours hit south-west ThailandThe monsoon season has officially begun in parts of Asia, marking the start of a period of enhanced rainfall vital to the region’s economy.The south-west monsoon begins each year as a consequence of a growing temperature difference between the Asian land mass and the Indian Ocean. Through spring, the land heats up more rapidly than the

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.
